key_insight: Lean can act as a process-level reward oracle, giving dense, verified tactic-by-tactic feedback for reinforcement learning in theorem proving instead of only a final pass/fail signal.
engineering_relevance: This makes formal-proof training more sample-efficient and trustworthy by aligning language-model optimization with type-theoretic verification throughout the proof process.
method: Parse proof attempts into Lean tactic sequences.
Use Lean elaboration to label locally sound steps and identify the first failing step.
Train with a GRPO-style objective using first-error propagation and first-token credit to mix outcome-level and process-level advantages.
evidence_signal: Experiments on STP-Lean and DeepSeek-Prover-V1.5 reportedly show tactic-level supervision beats outcome-only baselines in most settings, with gains on MiniF2F and ProofNet.
watch_item: The approach depends on reliable proof parsing and elaboration traces, so engineering complexity and prover-specific integration could limit portability.
paper_title: Process-Verified Reinforcement Learning for Theorem Proving via Lean
